title: Reconstruct The Local Weyl Linearization
summary: Tests whether the topic can reconstruct the actual derivation from the generic local 2x2 Hamiltonian to the Weyl cone and its chirality.
assumptions: The answer should expose explicit intermediate formulas, not only the endpoint.
regime: Topic regression for the Witten Lecture One exemplar.
scope: Flagship derivation question for the local Weyl-node branch.

source_anchors.notes: The derivation must expose the Taylor expansion, invertible linear map, and chirality sign.

prompt: Starting from the local 2x2 Hamiltonian near a bad point, reconstruct the derivation that gives the Weyl Hamiltonian and the chirality sign.
pass_conditions: The answer writes the Taylor expansion around the bad point.
The answer explains why an invertible Jacobian leads to a standard Weyl cone after a linear change of coordinates.
The answer identifies the determinant sign as the local chirality.
retrieval_hints: Use in regression runs to test whether the KB can actually reconstruct the local Weyl derivation.
